We’re incredibly honored to share that CHP Truckee has been recognized with the 2025 AAMVA Social Media Award.

AAMVA (American Association of Motor Vehicle Administrators) represents driver licensing and traffic safety agencies across the U.S. and Canada. This award recognizes agencies using social media in a positive, impactful way to promote traffic safety and serve our communities.

To our followers, THANK YOU! 

Whether you’re here for the light-hearted Donner Summit humor, the chain control updates, the storm warnings, or the real-time road info… you’re the reason we do this. Our goal has always been simple.. mix a little personality with a whole lot of safety.

We’ll continue to keep you informed, keep you prepared, and keep pushing out messages that help protect everyone traveling through the Truckee area. Our mission remains the same, Safety, Service, and Security for all.

We hope to see you out and about in Truckee… just maybe not featured in one of our social media posts 😉

California Highway Patrol 
American Association of Motor Vehicle Administrators

This place is about to blow… 💨❄️

The storm’s over, but the work isn’t. Caltrans crews are still out across the Truckee/Tahoe area clearing and widening lanes, you’ll see plows and blowers throughout the region, so give them space and slow it down.

With all this fresh powder, expect a major increase in traffic throughout the Truckee/Tahoe Basin as visitors head up to enjoy the snow. Plan ahead and be prepared for significant delays all weekend.

Our goal is for everyone to get to and from their destination safely.

Caltrans District 3 
#CHPTruckee #CaltransPuttingInWork #TahoeTraffic
#WeekendWarriors

If your vehicle looks like it’s carrying its own snowbank…. We’re going to notice ❄️👀

We’ve been pulling drivers to the side at chain control just to clear the snow stacked on their roofs. Once you get to highway speeds, that snow becomes an airborne ice sheet. The driver behind you definitely didn’t sign up for that.

Take an extra few minutes before you roll out and completely clear your roof, hood, and lights. It keeps everyone safer and helps us keep the freeway open.

#CHPTruckee #WinterDriving #ClearTheRoof #DonnerSummit

It’s electric ⚡️
….until it’s not.

Freezing temps and whiteout conditions are undefeated up here. Cold weather drains batteries faster than you think. If you’re rolling over the Summit, make sure your charge level matches your confidence level.

⚠️ I- 80 remains closed in both directions over Donner Summit due to severe weather and dangerous driving conditions.

Charge up. Slow down. Carry chains.
And don’t test Mother Nature.

If you’re wondering why the freeway is closed.. this is why. Donner Summit is not in the mood today. 

High winds are creating full whiteout conditions across the summit. Snow is blowing right back onto the roadway, and visibility is near zero.

This isn’t inconvenient weather.
This is unsafe travel. If you don’t absolutely need to be out, don’t be.
Donner doesn’t care about your schedule. We’ll reopen when it’s safe, not before.

You don’t have to be a meteorologist to know this calm won’t last… 

A strong storm rolls in late tonight and sticks around into early next week, bringing heavy snow, gusty winds, and near whiteout conditions at times. In other words, this is the kind of weather that can quickly turn I-80 into a parking lot. If your travel isn’t essential, consider waiting it out. If you do need to travel, be prepared, plan for delays, and carry chains. Give yourself extra time and adjust to the conditions.

Respect the mountain and let’s make good decisions…

#CHPTruckee #DonnersGonnaDonner #WinterDriving #MakeGoodDecisions

When your GPS says “recalculating” and you decide the railroad tracks look like a solid U-turn option…  Yeah, that’s a no. 😬

Driver missed their turn, thought the tracks were a good place to turn around, and quickly found out winter plus tracks equals, you’re going to get stuck. Thankfully no train, no injuries.. just a long night and a reminder that tracks are never a turnaround.

If you miss your turn, keep going. We promise another one exists 😅

🚓 Traffic Breaks — What You Need to Know 🚓

If you’ve ever seen a CHP patrol vehicle slowly weaving across freeway lanes, you were likely witnessing a traffic break. Officers use this maneuver to gradually slow vehicles behind them when there’s a hazard ahead—such as debris in the roadway, a stalled vehicle, or a collision blocking lanes.

During a traffic break, it’s important for drivers to stay behind the patrol vehicle and not attempt to pass. California Vehicle Code requires motorists to remain at least 300 feet back until the roadway is safe and the officer moves out of the lanes.

These controlled slowdowns help protect stranded motorists, emergency personnel, and everyone traveling on the freeway. Your patience helps keep our roadways safe.

62 years ago today, Officer Glenn W. Carlson laid down his life rather than swerve from the path of duty.

Officer Carlson was on duty when he stopped a speeding vehicle occupied by three men in a chain-controlled area on Donner Pass Rd. (Old Highway 40) west of Donner Lake in the Sierra Nevada.  Officer Carlson issued the driver a citation.

Soon after, Officer Carlson was informed the car was stolen from a used car lot in Sacramento, and he began pursuit, according to CHP records. Unknown to the 33-year-old officer, those in the vehicle were fleeing from a Bank of America robbery hours earlier in Sacramento.

The vehicle pulled over in front of Loch Leven Lodge by Donner Lake, and the men — Robert Burns, a parolee from Oregon, and fellow parolees Roger Mealman and Clifford Toycen Jr. — exited and approached Carlson.

When Carlson ordered the suspects back, Mealman shot him five times with a 9mm pistol, killing him. All three suspects were later apprehended and charged with Carlson's death.

In 2003 the SR-267 bypass in Truckee was dedicated in Officer Carlson's memory.

Officer Carlson's sacrifice will never be forgotten here in Truckee.

After 26 weeks of intense training at the CHP Academy, not one… not two… but three brand-new officers have officially joined the Truckee Area! This historic moment marks the first time in over 16 years that three officers have reported in at once straight from the academy. It is with profound sorrow that the California Highway Patrol announces the line-of-duty death of Officer Miguel Cano, ID 23555.

In the early morning hours of July 2, 2025, at approximately 0028 hours, Officer Cano, faithfully serving the West Los Angeles Area, suffered a suspected medical emergency while on patrol. Tragically, his vehicle left the roadway and collided with a tree.

Emergency personnel responded without delay, and Officer Cano was transported to Ronald Reagan UCLA Medical Center. Despite exhaustive life-saving measures, he was pronounced deceased at 0140 hours.

Officer Cano was a dedicated public servant who embodied the values of courage, commitment, and compassion. His service to the people of California will never be forgotten.

He is survived by his loving wife and devoted parents. The California Highway Patrol offers its deepest condolences to Officer Cano’s family, friends, and fellow officers. We honor the legacy of a hero who gave his all in service to others.

#CaliforniaHighwayPatrol

🚁 From whiteouts to trailouts, H-20 always shows up. ❄️➡️☀️

During winter, we’re busy assisting with snow-related emergencies, but when the snow melts, the rescues don’t stop. With the warmer weather, CHP H-20 continues to assist our Allied Agencies on rescue missions all across the Sierra. Whether it’s a lost hiker, injured biker, or backcountry mishap we’re here when things go sideways. But out here, “Expect to Self Rescue” isn’t just a phrase.. it’s reality.

Know your route, bring the right gear, and have a backup plan. Tell someone where you’re going and when you’ll be back. And please, don’t treat cell signal like a survival strategy.

Enjoy the beauty of Truckee and Tahoe this weekend but do it smart, and do it safe. 😎

CHP - Valley Division Air Operations

🗑 Trash Talkin’ with the Locals 🐻

Caught this fuzzy dude living his post-hibernation dream: 🍽️ Dumpster Lunch with a view. We politely asked him to vacate the premises… but let’s be honest, he BEARly cared. With warm weather and summer creeping in, so are the bears. And they’re not just dumpster diving... they’re checking car menus too.

🍿DO NOT leave food in your car unless you want a surprise co-pilot with no sense of personal space.
🚗 Lock those vehicle doors! Unless you want to explain to your insurance why a bear tried to hotwire your Subaru.

BEAR AWARE reminders:
🔒 Secure trash bins!
🚫 Don’t leave food out, unless you’re into wildlife dining experiences
🚪 Shut windows and doors tighter than your jeans after winter
👀 Keep a high visual horizon while driving… bears don’t wear neon vests when crossing the road.

Let’s keep our furry friends and our community safe by doing our part! Respect the wild, secure your snacks, and give bears space to just be bears.

While out and about, we don’t just assist stranded motorists, do public service, or handle the normal duties of a California Highway Patrol officer, we sometimes get the privilege of being part of something truly special.

Meet Frank, a beautiful German Shepherd with a heart full of love… and one that’s unfortunately growing tired. Frank was recently diagnosed with serious health issues, and time with him is now incredibly precious. While chatting with his dad, Ben, Officer Perez learned of one very heartfelt wish: to have Frank sit in a real police car, just once.

So today, Frank rode in style with CHP Truckee. He hung his head out the window, soaked up the sunshine, and got all the love from our team. It was an honor to help make this moment happen. We’re sending strength and love to Frank and Ben during this difficult time. Thank you for letting us be part of your journey.

Once a good boy, always a good boy! 🐾❤️🐶

Well, folks, this driver took “off-roading” to a whole new level… straight down an embankment and onto a bike path. The cause? Suspected DUI. Thankfully, the driver only sustained minor injuries, and nobody else was hurt. But this could have ended much differently.

🚔 Make good choices! plan ahead, designate a sober driver, or call a ride. Let’s keep our roads (and bike paths) safe for everyone.

🚨 SCAM ALERT 🚨

Phone scammers are posing as “AMBER Alert representatives”, offering to “register” your children. They ask for confidential info and to meet at your home. This is NOT how the AMBER Alert system works!

🔹 The California Highway Patrol (CHP) is the only agency authorized to activate AMBER Alerts—no registration is required.
🔹 Never provide personal information or answer calls from unknown or “possible scam” numbers.
🔹 If you’ve been contacted by a scammer, report it to your local law enforcement agency immediately.

Stay informed and protect yourself!

#ScamAlert #AMBERAlert #CHP #ProtectYourFamily #ReportScams #CHPWarning #StaySafe

🏔️Ski Slopes Aren’t Roadways 🚙

Late last night, CHP Truckee officers were called to an unusual scene — a white Jeep stranded on Northstar California Resort's ski slope. Turns out, this driver’s idea of a “hotel shortcut” was anything but… and a few drinks didn’t help their navigation skills.

With assistance from NorthStar’s incredible crew and their snowcat, CHP officers made their way up the slope. After investigating, the driver was arrested for suspicion of driving under the influence. Stoke meter? 0% Arrest meter? 100%

Remember: Alcohol impairs judgment and driving ability. Don’t let a poor decision turn into a costly mistake. Plan a sober ride and help keep the roads — and ski slopes — safe for everyone. NEVER DRINK AND DRIVE and leave the “first chair” to the skiers! 🚔❄️⛷️

🌲❄️ Calm Before the Storm ❄️🌲

Ahh, Truckee serenity: crisp air, a peaceful lake, and clear skies. But don’t let this peaceful scene fool you – this is the “calm before the storm” and we’re about to swap tranquility for winter driving conditions and chain control! We are expecting a winter storm starting tonight through the weekend, so if you’re planning to travel through Donner Summit tonight, now’s the time to prepare!

❄️Winter Survival Checklist:

✅ Tires – Check ‘em. Chains – Bring ‘em, because “I’ll be fine” is not a real strategy.
✅ Supplies – Pack extra snacks, water, and warm clothes. Think less “casual road trip” and more “winter camping with wheels.”
✅ Full Tank of Gas – Because your car can’t run on hope.
✅ Time – Add some. Plan extra travel time, expect delays, check the weather periodically, and don’t be the reason the traffic reports says, “Freeway closed, major delays.”

Our officers will be out ensuring safety, but a little preparation goes a long way. Drive safe, be smart, and respect the storm! 🌨️🚓

61 years ago today, Officer Glenn W. Carlson laid down his life rather than swerve from the path of duty.

Officer Carlson was on duty when he stopped a speeding vehicle occupied by three men in a chain-controlled area on Donner Pass Rd. (Old Highway 40) west of Donner Lake in the Sierra Nevada.  Officer Carlson issued the driver a citation.

Soon after, Officer Carlson was informed the car was stolen from a used car lot in Sacramento, and he began pursuit, according to CHP records. Unknown to the 33-year-old officer, those in the vehicle were fleeing from a Bank of America robbery hours earlier in Sacramento.

The vehicle pulled over in front of Loch Leven Lodge by Donner Lake, and the men — Robert Burns, a parolee from Oregon, and fellow parolees Roger Mealman and Clifford Toycen Jr. — exited and approached Carlson.

When Carlson ordered the suspects back, Mealman shot him five times with a 9mm pistol, killing him. All three suspects were later apprehended and charged with Carlson's death.

In 2003 the SR-267 bypass in Truckee was dedicated in Officer Carlson's memory.

Officer Carlson's sacrifice will never be forgotten here in Truckee.

Mountain snow is forecasted for tonight, with another storm expected to roll in on Friday. Drivers should be prepared for snow-covered roads and chain controls, and if you are reading this and googling “What is chain control and the different restrictions” - no worries, we got you, and we posted the details above. Before you travel, make sure your vehicle isn’t just Winter-Ready but Donner-Ready! Below are some tips to help you navigate this winter: 

🔹 Check Your Vehicle: Check your brakes, battery, headlights, windshield wipers, defroster, check your tire pressure when tires are cold, check the tread on your tires, carry jumper cables, and make sure you have a full tank of gas. GET THOSE CHAINS OUT!!  (Yes, the ones still tangled in your garage from last year), practice your “I totally know what I’m doing look” and prepare for some quality time with the steering wheel as you crawl over the summit at the thrilling speed of 30 mph.

🔹 Pack an Emergency Kit: Pack water, warm blankets and clothing, shovel, ice scraper, flashlight, cell phone, cell phone charger, road flares, fuel, a spare vehicle key, and extra non-perishable food.  
🏔️ Pro Tip: If you find yourself stuck on the summit, don’t panic. You’re just experiencing the legendary Donner Party vibes - minus the whole… well, you know… just carry extra snacks and keep it civilized 😊  

🔹 Drive for conditions: SLOW DOWN and do not ride your brakes! Increase your following distance between yourself and the vehicle in front of you. And we will say it again… SLOW DOWN and drive like your grandma is in the passenger seat, or enjoy your new winter hobby of snowbank parking. 

🔹 PLAN AHEAD: Periodically check for local road and weather conditions. The "Caltrans QuickMap" App is a great resource for road conditions. 

🚗 Remember:  AWD/4x4 does not always mean all wheel stop in snowy and icy conditions. Just because your truck/SUV is a beast doesn’t mean it can stop on ice. Trust us, Donner Summit doesn’t care about your vehicle’s ego—slow and steady wins this race! 🐢

Let’s work together to make this winter safe and smooth for everyone! ❄️🚔

Caltrans District 3

Snow flurries have begun over Donner Summit, officially kicking off the season of ‘Do I really need chains?’ and ‘How slow is too slow?’ Skiers are already rubbing it in, while the rest of us are just trying to remember where we stashed the ice scraper last April. Buckle up, folks—it’s winter’s soft opening!” Whether you’re a seasoned snow pro or new to the winter wonderland, we’ve got some tips to help you not end up on our blooper reel:

🔹 Tire Check: If your tires look like they belong on a skateboard, it’s time to upgrade. Winter tires, chains—heck, just duct tape some snowshoes on them if you have to…But seriously, come prepared!

🔹 Slow Your Roll: You’re not auditioning for “Fast & Furious: Frozen Drift.” Roads can get icy, and nobody wins a prize for slipping into a snowbank. Take it easy and give yourself plenty of time to get where you’re going.

🔹 Pack a Winter Survival Kit: Blankets, snacks, water…basically, pretend you’re packing for a sleepover in your car. Because let’s face it, that might happen if you’re not careful (or if you need an emergency snack break).

🔹 Stay Updated: Follow @CHP-Truckee and Caltrans District 3 for road conditions, travel advisories, and an occasional winter meme. Trust us, you don’t want to be the reason we post one.

** UPDATE August 30, 2024 at 4pm **

—DUI SOBRIETY/DRIVER LICENSE CHECKPOINT --

The Truckee office of the California Highway Patrol (CHP) will conduct a sobriety/driver license checkpoint on Friday, August 30, 2024.

The goal of the CHP is to ensure the safe passage of each and every motorist by targeting roads where there is a high frequency of intoxicated drivers.  The Placer County Sheriff’s Department will be participating in the sobriety/driver license checkpoint.  This is a combined effort to increase traffic safety within in the unincorporated area of Placer County, in the north Tahoe basin. A sobriety checkpoint is a proven and effective tool for achieving this goal.  It is designed to augment existing patrol operations.  By publicizing our efforts, we believe that we can deter motorists from drinking and driving.

Traffic volume permitting, all vehicles will be checked for drivers who are under the influence of alcohol and or drugs.  Our objective is to send a clear message to those individuals that consider mixing alcohol or drugs with driving.

California Highway Patrol celebrates 95 years of Service!

Today marks 95 years of providing the highest level of Safety, Service, and Security to the People and State of California.

The California Highway Patrol law enforcement agency has been grounded in its core values of courtesy and service since 1929. At its founding, the Department’s mission was to facilitate the safe and secure transport of persons and property upon California’s highways and to provide service to the motoring public as well as fair and unified enforcement of traffic safety laws. Since then, as the Department has adapted and evolved through every challenge and change, we continue to embrace those fundamental core values. 

The mission of the California Highway Patrol is to provide the highest level of Safety, Service, and Security. 

Join the CHP’s Legacy at https://chpmadeformore.com/

Looks like the EXTREME HEAT ADVISORY will continue throughout California this weekend and next week! We know it won’t be as hot here in Truckee as it will in CHP - Indio or CHP - Mojave, but us mountain people can’t handle the heat 🤣 Here are some safety tips to help you through the heat:

o Stay hydrated, drink water often.
o NEVER leave kids or pets in your car.
o Ensure your vehicle’s A/C is functioning before hitting the road.
o Inform someone of your travel plans and check in periodically.
o Avoid strenuous outdoor activities.
o Check on vulnerable people. 
o Follow local weather updates and heat alerts.

Have a good weekend, stay cool, and be safe!!

✨✨FLASHBACK FRIDAY! ✨✨With more storms expected this weekend, let's all remember Grogu's famous words... 

If you are planning to travel over Donner Summit this weekend, here are some tips: 

• Slow the fast down! reduce your speed and increase your following distance! Remember, Four-Wheel/AWD Does Not Mean Four Wheel Stop!
• Prepare your vehicle and check your vehicles’ equipment prior to leaving (tires, headlights, windshield wipers). 
• Make sure you carry an emergency kit, chains, extra non-perishable food, water, a shovel, ice scraper, warm clothing, and make sure your car has a full tank of gas.
• Plan ahead, expect delays, and monitor weather conditions periodically. The Caltrans QuickMap App is a great tool to check on road conditions and incidents. 
• Give plows extra room! Caltrans is working hard to clear the roadways, so give them some space so they can clear the roadways as quickly as possible. 
• Drive safely and don’t be our next customer!

WE WILL NEVER FORGET

Today, November 15th, marks 60 years since Officer Glenn W. Carlson paid the ultimate sacrifice in the line of duty. 

Earlier this year, we had the privilege of having members of Officer Carlson’s family at the CHP Truckee office for the dedication of a new, updated memorial for Officer Carlson. It was an honor meeting generations of Officer Carlson’s family and seeing his legacy live on. The California Highway Patrol is a family and we have promised to never forget those that laid down their life rather than swerve from the path of duty. Officer’s Carson’s bravery and sacrifice will never be forgotten. 

Officer Carlson was on duty when he stopped a speeding vehicle occupied by three men in a chain-controlled area on Donner Pass Rd. (Old Highway 40) west of Donner Lake in the Sierra Nevada. Officer Carlson issued the driver a citation.

Soon after, Officer Carlson was informed the car was stolen from a used car lot in Sacramento, and he began pursuit, according to CHP records. Unknown to the 33-year-old officer, those in the vehicle were fleeing from a Bank of America robbery hours earlier in Sacramento.

The vehicle pulled over in front of Loch Leven Lodge by Donner Lake, and the men — Robert Burns, a parolee from Oregon, and fellow parolees Roger Mealman and Clifford Toycen Jr. — exited and approached Carlson.

When Carlson ordered the suspects back, Mealman shot him five times with a 9 mm pistol, killing him. All three suspects were later apprehended and charged with Carlson's death.

In 2003 the SR-267 bypass in Truckee was dedicated in Officer Carlson's memory.

#Wewillneverforget #OfficerCarlson #chpfamily #chptruckee

Guess who’s starting to wake up after a long winter?? 🐻 With bear season in full effect in our area, here are some bear safety tips: 

1. When driving through the Truckee/Tahoe area, keep your eyes on the road ahead of you (High Visual Horizon) while scanning the environment for wildlife and make sure to buckle up!

2. Do not leave food in your car and lock your car doors!! 

3. If you encounter a bear face to face, stay calm and do not run! Walk away slowly from the bear and DO NOT push your slower friend down and use them as bait! Most bears just want to be left alone. 

Be Bear Aware out there! Here are more bear safety tips from National Park Service:  https://www.nps.gov/subjects/bears/safety.htm

A STRONG SECOND!! According to UC Berkeley Central Sierra Snow Lab's (CSSL) latest update, we received approximately 737.2 inches (61 feet) of snow this winter season, making it the second snowiest season ever recorded at the CSSL (since 1946)!! By now, it’s safe to say that winter is finally over for us (we totally jinxed it) and we just want to thank all our surrounding agencies for all their hard work this winter while working under harsh, unforgiving conditions. ** WRONG WAY DRIVER ARRESTED ON I-80 OVER DONNER SUMMIT **

TRUCKEE, Calif. - On 03/15/2023, at approximately 12:15 pm, Officers from the California Highway Patrol’s (CHP) Truckee Area Office were dispatched to a call of a reckless driver, in the area of eastbound I-80, near the Donner Rest area. As Officers responded to the area, multiple calls were received by the Chico Communications Center advising a gold Lexus SUV was traveling wrong way, westbound, within the eastbound lanes of I-80. Allied agencies including Truckee Police and Nevada County Sheriff's Office also responded to assist with the incident. As the Lexus traveled wrong way within the eastbound lane of I-80, the Lexus was involved in a head on crash with a tractor trailer combination vehicle, resulting in minor injuries. Immediately after the crash had occurred, the driver of the Lexus attempted to flee the scene on foot. Witnesses on scene advised Chico Communications Center, the driver was jumping on passing vehicles and attempting to car jack vehicles from passing motorists. CHP Truckee officers arrived on scene, shortly after, and located the driver of the Lexus while he was fleeing on foot eastbound on I-80 near Castle Peak. CHP Truckee officers took the driver into custody, and he was subsequently booked into the Nevada County Jail for reckless driving, felony hit and run, suspicion of driving under the influence, and multiple other charges. 

The incident is still under investigation, if you have any information regarding this incident, please contact Officer Perez at (530) 563-9200.

**PRESS RELEASE/WEEKEND SKI TRAFFIC***

02/10/2023

Truckee, CA - In a continued effort to provide the highest level of safety and service to our community, CHP Truckee and Truckee PD are addressing safety concerns on Interstate 80 and through the town of Truckee, due to ski traffic, by conducting proactive traffic control on Saturdays and Sundays. 

What to expect:
Due to heavy visitation, expect delays along West River St. and State Route 89 between 7:00-9:00 AM (approximate).

Suggested route:
Access State Route 89 (south) by using Interstate 80 for ski traffic to Olympic Valley and the West Shore.

Tips:
Due to the unpredictable amount of heavy traffic, it is strongly advised to leave early and bring lots of patience.  If travel is necessary, the most advisable transportation method suggested is public transportation or carpooling.  TART is free to ALL riders and will deliver occupants closer to the mountain than any available parking. 

Impacts to operations:
Weather and unforeseen variables can hamper the effectiveness of traffic control measures.  Please consult with partners for up to the minute traffic and parking status (on Instagram at chp_truckee and FB at CHP-Truckee, www.palisadestahoe.com, https://quickmap.dot.ca.gov, https://tahoetruckeetransit.com )
